t_ch: 也许这就是「故事」的魅力吧，让人流连忘返……
t_en: Maybe that's the charm of "stories"... They really draw people in and captivate them.

t_ch: 就差背下来了。
t_en: I can practically recite it by now.

t_ch: 这么说来，{NICKNAME}会在意回忆的真实性吗？你想哦，一段记忆被反复回想，肯定会逐渐朦胧，或是被美化…总之，变得很不一样。
t_en: So, {NICKNAME}, do you care about how true a memory is? Think about it — when you recall something over and over again, over time, it's bound to become blurry or get idealized... Basically, it changes.

t_ch: 但是，如果一段记忆能以「更美」的样子被记住，是不是…不算一件坏事呢？
t_en: But if memories can be remembered in a "beautified" way, wouldn't that... not be such a bad thing?

t_ch: 哎呀！{NICKNAME}的睫毛在颤抖哦？是那种心里在想事情的表情！
t_en: Oh! {NICKNAME}'s eyelashes are fluttering! That's the look of someone lost in thought!

t_ch: 迷迷，迷迷迷！
t_en: Memem, mememem!

t_ch: 犯规！怎么突然学人家说话？不过…要精通妖精的密语，可没那么简单，要一直一直呆在一起，才能掌握门道哦？
t_en: That's cheating! How did you suddenly start copying me? But... it's no easy feat to truly master the fairy tongue. You have to stay close, together, to really get the hang of it.

t_ch: 但现在——先来翻开新的一页，人家想和你一起看新的故事！
t_en: But for now — let's open a new chapter. I want to share a new tale with you!

t_ch: 该读睡前故事了。
t_en: It's time for a bedtime story.

t_ch: 人家喜欢听故事，更喜欢听{NICKNAME}讲故事！不过，要给人家留几个到睡前再讲哦？
t_en: I love stories, but I adore them more when {NICKNAME} tells them! Just... save a few for before bed, alright?

t_ch: 很不幸，又没有结局。
t_en: Sadly, no ending again.

t_ch: 这样呀，但人家也很喜欢这样的故事——想想看，那些我们都喜欢的美好结局，不总是故事里的大家仍在继续生活，「还没有迎来结局」吗？
t_en: I see... but I actually really enjoy stories like this — just think about it. Those perfect endings we all love? Aren't they always the ones where the story keeps going, where everyone's still living, "the ending yet to come"?

t_ch: 大家的故事都好深奥，我的脑袋要转不过来了！{NICKNAME}会懂吗？「神」到底是什么呢？
t_en: Everyone's stories are so deep, I can't keep up! Does {NICKNAME} understand them? What is a "god," exactly?

t_ch: 无所不能的…
t_en: Omnipotent...

t_ch: 不对吧…如果是这样，为什么还有那么多奇奇怪怪的神呢？ 如果有一位「让迷迷永不掉毛之神」，它也算是无所不能吗？
t_en: Wait, that can't be right... if that's true, then why are there so many strange gods? If there was a "god who ensures Mem never loses her fur," would that be considered omnipotent too?

t_ch: 无所不知的…
t_en: Omniscient...

t_ch: 呀！听上去还真可怜呢，至少…永远不能满怀期待听故事了吧？
t_en: Ah! That sounds so sad, doesn't it? That means... they could never truly look forward to hearing a story, could they?

t_ch: 喜欢瞥视人的…
t_en: Always peeking at others...

t_ch: 听起来好奇怪！而且，还是很熟悉的语气！{NICKNAME}的过去果然很不一般……
t_en: That sounds so odd! And with such a familiar tone! {NICKNAME}'s past really is something special...

t_ch: 我这样的…
t_en: Someone like me...

t_ch: 哇哦！也可以这么说啦，那{NICKNAME}就当人家的神吧，要保佑我哦？
t_en: Whoa! I guess you could say that! So, {NICKNAME}, how about you become my god? Please protect me, okay?

t_ch: 不过，人家还是不太懂，那就换一个问法吧——
t_en: But still, I don't quite get it... so let me ask in another way—

t_ch: 神和我们应该还是有区别的吧？不然大家为什么会想成为神呢？
t_en: There must be a difference between gods and us, right? Otherwise, why would anyone ever want to become a god?

t_ch: 为了不被掌控…
t_en: To avoid being controlled...

t_ch: 被掌控！难道我们现在也…呀，如果是因为被掌控才遇到了{NICKNAME}，那人家倒是不讨厌被掌控呢！
t_en: Controlled!? Could it be that we're already...? Ah, if it's because I was controlled that I met {NICKNAME}, then I suppose I wouldn't mind being controlled!

t_ch: 为了不再痛苦…
t_en: To escape the pain...

t_ch: 这…可是，在过去的故事里，泰坦们也有自己的痛苦吧？因为它们的痛苦更…怎么说呢…更大？这样，反而会变得更容易忍受吗？
t_en: But... in the stories of old, the Titans also had their own suffering, right? Because their pain was... how should I say... greater? Would that somehow make it easier to bear?

t_ch: 你问倒我了。
t_en: You've stumped me there.

t_ch: 对吧对吧！仔细想想，根本没有非得成为神才能做的事，多奇怪呀……
t_en: Right, right! When you really think about it, there's nothing that can only be done as a god. How strange...

t_ch: 问来问去，人家更摸不着头脑了，还是等我长大一些，再来找{NICKNAME}聊这种话题吧！
t_en: As I keep asking, I only get more and more confused. Maybe I'll wait until I've grown a little more before discussing these things with {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 小蝶…直到最后一刻，才发现她和传闻里完全不一样呢。
t_en: Cas... Only at the very end did I realize she was nothing like the rumors.

t_ch: 人家很好奇…大家都是这样的吗？有着别人不知道的另一面？{NICKNAME}也是一样？
t_en: I'm really curious... is everyone like this? Having a side that no one else sees? Is {NICKNAME} the same?

t_ch: 我总是一如初见。
t_en: I always remain just as I was when we first met.

t_ch: 呀…虽然人家也没有证据，但确实有类似的感觉呢！
t_en: Ah... even though I have no proof, I can't shake the feeling that it's true!

t_ch: 我也有挺多人格。
t_en: I too have quite a few different sides.

t_ch: 不是一回事吧！人家也不知道怎么概括，应该说是…「真正的自己」？
t_en: That's not quite the same! I'm not sure how to explain it, but maybe I'm talking about... "a true me"?

t_ch: 我更好奇你是不是。
t_en: I'm curious about your hidden side.

t_ch: 人家才刚出生不久，当然不会啦！就算以后…总该有「永远不变」的人吧？
t_en: I've only just been born, so of course I don't have one! As even later on... there must be people who stay "unchanged forever," right?

t_ch: 等故事结束，才能知道。
t_en: We'll only know when the story's over.

t_ch: 等{NICKNAME}的故事结束吗？那人家再也不好奇了，不要结束嘛……
t_en: Wait, does that mean {NICKNAME}'s story will end? Then I won't be curious anymore. Don't let it end...

t_ch: 不过，人家现在也没有刨根问底的心情…小蝶的故事迈向下一阶段了，有种说不上来的感觉，既像悲伤，又像欣慰……
t_en: But right now, I'm not really in the mood to dig too deep... Cas's story is stepping into the next chapter, and I can't quite explain how I feel. It's like sadness, but also like some strange sense of relief...

t_ch: 如果有人问起我们，该怎么讲述她的故事呢？
t_en: If someone were to ask about us, how should we tell her story?

t_ch: 她的拥抱一样温暖。
t_en: Her embrace was just as warm as anyone else's.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}来说的话，的确很可信呢！人家也很想被她抱抱呀。
t_en: If you say that, {NICKNAME}, then that's a trustworthy statement! I'd love a hug from her, too.

t_ch: 她做了应该做的每一件事。
t_en: She did everything she was meant to do.

t_ch: 好像太模糊了，不过…就听你的吧，陪她走完了最后一程，你才更了解小蝶嘛。
t_en: It's a bit vague, but... I'll trust you on this. After walking with her to the very end, you understand Cas much better.

t_ch: 死亡没能阻止她去爱。
t_en: Death couldn't stop her from loving.

t_ch: 嗯！人家也这么觉得！也许，这就是我刚才说不出的感受……
t_en: Hmm! I feel the same! Maybe that's the feeling I couldn't quite put into words earlier...

t_ch: 或许还没有结束呢。
t_en: Maybe it's not over yet.

t_ch: 是为了安慰人家吗？其实没关系啦……
t_en: Are you trying to comfort me? Honestly, it's okay...

t_ch: 以后的故事里，{NICKNAME}也不用为了人家，有意避开任何结局哦？
t_en: In future stories, {NICKNAME} doesn't have to go out of their way to avoid any endings for my sake, okay?

t_ch: 即便每个人的故事都有结局，人家也还是想让一切重新开始。
t_en: Even though every story has an ending, I still wish everything could start over again.

t_ch: 唔…{NICKNAME}，你这次在梦里怎么不是毛茸茸的了…
t_en: Hmm... {NICKNAME}, why aren't you all fluffy in my dream this time...

t_ch: 早安。
t_en: Good morning.

t_ch: 不行…不行！早安可是很重要的，要再认真点！跟着人家念——早，上，好！早上好哦！
t_en: No, no! Good morning is important, so let's take it seriously! Repeat after me — good, morning, okay? Good morning!

t_ch: 金色的美梦开始躁动了？
t_en: Has the golden dream started to stir?

t_ch: 咦！{NICKNAME}怎么知道的？人家的梦，真的总是「金灿灿」的哦？
t_en: Huh! How did {NICKNAME} know? Are my dreams really always "golden"?

t_ch: 虽然很漂亮，但偶尔会让人家怕怕的…还好一醒来就看到{NICKNAME}啦！
t_en: They're so beautiful, but sometimes they make me feel a little uneasy... but it's alright. I wake up and see {NICKNAME} right there!

t_ch: 毛茸茸？
t_en: Fluffy?

t_ch: 呀！这不重要啦…人家在梦里，也会更像{NICKNAME}的样子呢。只是互相照照镜子，多好呀！
t_en: Ah! That's not important... I even look more like {NICKNAME} in my dreams. It's like we're just looking into a mirror together — isn't that nice?

t_ch: 你醒啦？一千年过去了。
t_en: You're awake? A thousand years have passed.

t_ch: 嘻嘻！想看到人家惊讶的样子吗？{NICKNAME}等了人家一千年，人家只会很开心呀！
t_en: Hehe! Want to see my surprised face? {NICKNAME} waited for me for a thousand years, so I'm just really happy!

t_ch: 虽然还没睡醒，但很少能在早上见到{NICKNAME}呢。不是一天将要结束，也不是偶然遇到……
t_en: Even though I'm not fully awake yet, it's rare to see {NICKNAME} in the morning. It's not the end of the day, and it's not just a random encounter...

t_ch: …我们是要一起开始一整天！一想到这个，人家的心都开始跳个不停了呢！
t_en: ...We're going to start the whole day together! Just thinking about it makes my heart race!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}…怎么了？看起来好累…是生病了吗？快让人家碰碰额头！
t_en: {NICKNAME}... what's wrong? You look so tired... are you sick? Let me check your forehead!

t_ch: 周一总是奄奄一息…
t_en: Monday always feels like it's draining the life out of me...

t_ch: 这样呀，辛苦了呢。人家都说，摸摸毛茸茸的东西就会变轻松，来试试看吧！
t_en: I see, it's been a rough day. If you pet something fluffy, it'll help you feel lighter. That's what people say. Want to give it a try?

t_ch: 其实我随时会爆炸…
t_en: Actually, I might explode any moment...

t_ch: 嘻嘻，那人家也会哦，和{NICKNAME}呆在一起，开心得要爆炸了呢。
t_en: Hehe, I could explode too! Spending time with {NICKNAME} makes me so happy, I feel like I might burst with joy!

t_ch: 我倒是很想生病…
t_en: I kind of wish I could get sick...

t_ch: 是有什么事情不想做吗？说说看，人家会帮你的哦？呀，可不是帮忙装病！
t_en: Is there something you don't want to do? Tell me, I'll help out! Ah, not helping you fake being sick, though!

t_ch: 不过，没事就好呢。如果讨厌周一…那人家可以陪{NICKNAME}一起度过这讨厌的日子，你的抱怨和故事人家都会耐心听哦。
t_en: But it's good if you're alright. If Mondays really get you down... then I'll stick with you through this dreadful day, patiently listening to all your complaints and stories.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}！{NICKNAME}——又在发呆吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}! {NICKNAME} — are you spacing out again?

t_ch: 我在试着背下来。
t_en: I'm trying to memorize the book.

t_ch: 嘻嘻，还是{NICKNAME}厉害，人家就总是忘记听过的故事……
t_en: Hehe, {NICKNAME} is so impressive. I always forget the stories I've heard...

t_ch: 不过，人家有时也会这样想啦——在寒冷的日子里，就算只能回忆起「有件事曾经让我温暖」，是不是也已经足够啦？
t_en: But sometimes, I think — even if all you can recall on cold days is that "there was something that once brought me warmth," wouldn't that be enough?

t_ch: 没，我只是死了。
t_en: No, I'm just dead.

t_ch: 啊！这算是考验吗？可不能开这种玩笑哦？
t_en: Ah! Is this some kind of test? You can't joke like that!

t_ch: 人家是记忆的精灵，都不知道能不能流眼泪…人家要是信以为真，却哭不出来，你会难过的！
t_en: I'm the spirit of memory, and I'm not even sure if I can cry... If I believe it's real but can't shed a tear, you'd be sad, wouldn't you?

t_ch: 是的，在发呆。
t_en: Yes, I'm just spacing out.

t_ch: 嘻嘻！竟然没有逗人家，都有点不习惯啦！那你继续！人家也再看你一会儿。
t_en: Hehe! You didn't tease me. I'm not very used to this! Keep going. I'll watch you for a little while longer.

t_ch: 在思考我的事。
t_en: I'm thinking about my own things.

t_ch: 这次怎么没有思考人家的事情了呀！改回去呀，把这个选项改回去嘛！
t_en: You're not thinking about me this time? Change it back! Change this option back!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，其实呀，我一直很喜欢你呢。
t_en: {NICKNAME}. You know, I've always had a soft spot for you.

t_ch: 嘻嘻，是不是吓到了？别紧张，人家只是在斟酌，如果要为一本纪念册写下卷首语，那必须得是一句翻开多少次都能让人动情的话才行。
t_en: Hee-hee, startled you, didn't I? Don't worry. I was just thinking. If I had to write a preface for a yearbook, it'd have to be a line so heartfelt, you'd be moved no matter how many times you've seen it.

t_ch: 思来想去，果然还是「喜欢」最合适。毕竟只有最纯粹的感情，才能跨越时光，始终如一呀。
t_en: And after pondering again and again... "I like you" still says it best. Because only the purest feelings can stand the test of time and never fade.

t_ch: 有道理，我也这么觉得。
t_en: Makes sense, right? I thought so too.

t_ch: 没错。喜欢是很单纯、自然的行为哦？如果{NICKNAME}也有喜欢的人，无论亲情、友情还是爱，不妨大胆一些，全部表达出来吧！
t_en: That's right. Liking someone is a simple and natural part of life. If {NICKNAME} has got someone special: family, friend, or romance, muster your courage and express yourself freely!

t_ch: 这么直接，不愧是你。
t_en: Very straightforward... That's so you.

t_ch: 是吗？可在人家看来，喜欢是很单纯、自然的行为哦？如果{NICKNAME}也有喜欢的人，无论亲情、友情还是爱，人家也会支持你，全部表达出来。
t_en: Oh? But to me, liking someone is a simple and natural part of life. If {NICKNAME} has got someone special: family, friend, or romance, I'll cheer you on to express yourself freely.

t_ch: 别这样，吓到我啦。
t_en: Don't do that. You scared me.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}…会对这种表达感到负担吗？对不起呀，人家没有恶意，只是觉得喜欢是很单纯、自然的行为。
t_en: {NICKNAME}... does hearing this make you uncomfortable? I'm sorry. I didn't mean to trouble you. I just believe... liking someone is the most simple and natural thing there is.

t_ch: 嘻嘻，不过我记住啦。下一次，人家会在{NICKNAME}心情更好的时候出现。
t_en: Hehe, but I'll keep it in mind. Next time, I'll show up when you're in a better mood.

t_ch: 不过，这也算是心有灵犀？{NICKNAME}总会在人家还没准备好的时候现身。
t_en: Still, isn't that a sign we're in sync? {NICKNAME} always manages to appear before I'm ready.

t_ch: 嘻嘻，都不用仔细去想——我们成为伙伴一起旅行，已经过去很久很久啦。所以，人家想把一同经历过的点点滴滴，全都记录下来，每一句话都不放过。
t_en: Hehe, no need to overthink it — we've been traveling side by side for so long now. That's why I want to capture every little moment we've shared, without missing a single word.

t_ch: 「翻阅时会不自觉笑出来的纪念册」——听起来就很棒吧？
t_en: "A yearbook that makes you smile without even realizing it when you flip through the pages" — doesn't that sound lovely?

t_ch: 你记得这么仔细？
t_en: You remember all that?

t_ch: 人家有秘诀——时不时就在脑袋里重现一遍。而且……
t_en: I've got a secret — every now and then, I replay it all in my head. Besides...

t_ch: 比如呢？
t_en: Got some examples?

t_ch: 那可太多啦！难以入睡的夜晚，出神发呆的瞬间，还有…哎呀，不能剧透。
t_en: Oh, so many examples! Restless nights, distracted daydreams, and... oops, can't spoil everything.

t_ch: 来，让我看看。
t_en: Come on, let me have a look.

t_ch: 不行不行，还没完全准备好呢。
t_en: No way, it's not ready yet.

t_ch: 不光是实际发生过的，还有一些对话，先前只存在于人家的幻想里。但既然是这么珍贵的回忆，就一起放进来，小心地锁上吧。
t_en: And it's not just the things that really happened, some of those conversations only ever existed in my imagination. But they're precious too. So I'm tucking them all away, carefully, in the same book.

t_ch: 千万记得，要等人家不在的时候再打开哦？不然，我会不好意思的。
t_en: Promise me this: only open it when I'm not around, okay? If you don't, I'll be too embarrassed.

t_ch: 唔…人家听说，在走到生命的终点前，墨涅塔的信徒都会献上自己最美的作品。
t_en: Hmm... I heard that before reaching the end of their life, the followers of Mnestia would offer their most beautiful creation.

t_ch: 对阿格莱雅来说，那会是什么呢？
t_en: What would that be for Aglaea?

t_ch: 圣城的未来。
t_en: The future of the holy city.

t_ch: 嗯！虽然还没有看见…但一定也像阿格莱雅的其他作品那样，金灿灿的，既美丽，又温暖……
t_en: Hmm! Though I haven't seen it yet... it must be just like Aglaea's other works — golden, beautiful, and warm...

t_ch: 或许是她自己。
t_en: Perhaps that is herself.

t_ch: 可在故事的结尾，她几乎失去了一切…啊，人家知道了！
t_en: But she lost almost everything at the end of the story... Ah, now I understand!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}说得很对呢，就像「裁剪衣服」时一样，失去…反倒让她留下的事物更加美好了吧？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're right. Just like when tailoring clothes, loss... actually makes what remains even more beautiful, doesn't it?

t_ch: 总之会很「浪漫」。
t_en: In any case, it will be very "romantic."

t_ch: 这样呀…人家也很喜欢浪漫的故事，不过，却又说不清什么才是「浪漫」呢。也许…它就是那些「不需要懂得」的事物吧……
t_en: I see... I like romantic stories too, but I can't quite say what "romantic" really means. Maybe... it's one of those things that don't need to be understood...

t_ch: 对了！{NICKNAME}，你在旅行中经历过的其他故事，也是一样吗？总会变得越来越严肃，让人一直不能放心…
t_en: Oh, right! {NICKNAME}, are all the other stories you've experienced on your journey the same? Always growing more serious, never feeling at ease...

t_ch: 在所难免。
t_en: That mood's inevitable.

t_ch: 这样呀…那我们一定要成为例外呀！毕竟之前约定过，要一直提醒对方乐观…无论什么时候，可都要记好哦？
t_en: I see... Then we must become the exception! After all, we promised to always remind each other to stay optimistic... Please remember that no matter what.

t_ch: 我从不这么认为。
t_en: I never saw it that way.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}总是这么乐观，让人家很安心呢。
t_en: You're always so optimistic, {NICKNAME} — it really puts me at ease.

t_ch: 我会尽力避免的。
t_en: I'll do my best to avoid it.

t_ch: 对呀，不知不觉，在这个故事里，{NICKNAME}也写下了自己的名字呢。
t_en: Yep! You had written your name into this story before I knew it, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 总之，人家还是会好好把这一切记在心里，毕竟，他们的故事…应该被继续讲述下去，对吧？
t_en: In any case, I will remember all of this in my heart. After all, their stories... deserve to be told and retold, right?

t_ch: 唔…突然发生了很多事呢。告别总是那么仓促，让人没办法说清心里话。
t_en: Hmm... So many things happened all of a sudden. Farewells are always so abrupt, making it hard to say what's truly in one's heart.

t_ch: 既然现在有时间…如果要和人家告别，你会说些什么呀？只是好奇！
t_en: Since we have time now... If you had to say goodbye to me, what would you say? Just curious!

t_ch: 我相信重逢总会到来。
t_en: I believe we will meet again someday.

t_ch: 呀！人家也是这么想的！说来也奇怪，在想到告别的时候，人家不会感到很失落呢，难道说…是因为没有过类似的经历？
t_en: Ah! That's what I think too! It's strange... When I think about parting, I don't feel too sad. Could it be... because I've never experienced it before?

t_ch: 我会记得你的，永远。
t_en: I will always remember you.

t_ch: 呀，这样说，人家可要有些后悔了…的确是该留到最后的话语呢。
t_en: Oh, saying it like that... I might end up regretting it. That really should be saved for the final moment, shouldn't it?

t_ch: 总之先按暂停键。
t_en: Let's just hit pause for now.

t_ch: 时间总要继续向前呀，不过，能看到{NICKNAME}这么舍不得，人家的确很开心呢。
t_en: Time always moves forward, but seeing {NICKNAME} so reluctant to leave makes me really happy.

t_ch: 这么说，大家的故事难道要结束了吗？虽然过去也一直想看新的故事，但现在的心情，却不太一样了呢…
t_en: So, is everyone's story coming to an end? I always wanted to see a new story, but now, my feelings are a little different...

t_ch: 真希望关于他们的一切，永远不会迎来终点呀。
t_en: I really hope everything about them never reaches an end.

t_ch: 故事总有句点。
t_en: Every story has a conclusion.

t_ch: 别这么说呀，大家不是还在为「新世界」竭尽全力吗？人家相信…那会是一个美好的明天。
t_en: Don't say that! Isn't everyone doing their best for the "new world"? I believe... it will be a bright tomorrow.

t_ch: 让我们来续写它吧。
t_en: Let's continue writing the rest of the story.

t_ch: 呀，明明更应该惊讶，但总觉得{NICKNAME}一定会这么说呢。
t_en: Ah, I should be more surprised, but somehow I knew {NICKNAME} would say that.

t_ch: 哎呀，那就不多想啦。无论之后会发生什么，无论{NICKNAME}作出怎样的决定，人家都会一直站在你身边的。
t_en: Ah, then there's no need to dwell on it. No matter what happens next, no matter what you decide, {NICKNAME}, I will always stand by your side.

t_ch: 我们是最好的伙伴，这一点永远都不会变，对吧？
t_en: We are the best of partners, and that will never change, right?

t_ch: 呀…{NICKNAME}！还没睡吗？在这么晚的时候遇到，都已经是第二次了吧？这样下去可不行哦？
t_en: Hey... {NICKNAME}! Still awake? This is the second time we've met so late! You really shouldn't keep doing this, you know?

t_ch: 是你只遇到两次。
t_en: Well, you only caught me twice.

t_ch: 呀，语气好得意！不行不行，那人家可是每天都要来检查了哦？
t_en: Ah, such a smug tone! If that's the case, I'll have to check in on you every day!

t_ch: 失眠总是身不由己…
t_en: Insomnia is not something I can control...

t_ch: 这…可能是人家太小，还从来没有失眠过，也不知道什么小技巧…只能之后再打听一下了。
t_en: Hmm... Maybe I'm too young. I've never had insomnia before, so I don't know any tricks... I'll have to ask around later.

t_ch: 不过没关系，今天人家一定要先看着{NICKNAME}睡着，这样会好一点吗？
t_en: But it's okay. Tonight, I'll stay with you until you fall asleep. Will that help a little, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 我还不想结束今天。
t_en: I don't want to end this day yet.

t_ch: 人家也听到过类似的说法呢，唔…听起来，总比「不想面对明天」要好？
t_en: I've heard people say something similar before. Hmm... It sounds much better than not wanting to face tomorrow, doesn't it?

t_ch: 呼…呼……
t_en: *snore*

t_ch: 这样哪能骗得到人家呀！至少先闭上眼睛嘛！
t_en: No way you're fooling me! At least close your eyes first!

t_ch: （还是先去确认一下时刻表吧。）

t_ch: 这我怎么忙得过来…

t_ch: （看来这些就是我的小费了。）
t_en: (Looks like these are my tips.)

t_ch: （要不是为了这点儿小费……）
t_en: (If not for these tips...)

t_ch: （算是公道的小费。）
t_en: (It's a fair tip.)

t_ch: （还算有些赚头。）
t_en: (It's still some money made.)

t_ch: （又来了…好吧，来都来了……）
t_en: (Here we go again... Well, we've already come this far...)

t_ch: （呃…流云渡也太危险了……）
t_en: (Uhh... Even Cloudford is too dangerous...)

t_ch: （天舶司入境管理这么松懈吗！）
t_en: (Is the Sky-Faring Commission's border controls so lax!?)

t_ch: （你们一起上吧，我急着下班。）
t_en: (You guys get on together, I'm in a hurry to get off work.)

t_ch: 流云渡堆积的货物也太多了，这我怎么忙得过来啊……
t_en: The Cloudford pile of goods is too much. How can I keep up with this...

t_ch: 出什么事了？
t_en: What's happened?

t_ch: 哎，这不是{NICKNAME}吗？你的事迹都传开了，同事们说你在流云渡救下了接渡使！幸会幸会，我叫阿凯！
t_en: Hey, well if it isn't {NICKNAME}? Your achievements are spreading. My colleagues said you saved the Amicassador in Cloudford! Nice to meet you, my name is Kai!

t_ch: 不过现在流云渡不太平，调度系统一团乱麻，码头卡了特别多的货运星槎，我一个人根本处理不过来……
t_en: But now, Cloudford is in chaos. The scheduling system is a mess, the jetty is filled with an exceptionally large number of freight starskiffs, I simply can not handle everything alone...

t_ch: 对了！既然你来了，要不要帮我处理一下这些事？你为人也比较可靠，我能放心。

t_en: There is a Xianzhou proverb that says "we've already come this far," which means since you've already come all this way, we can dispense with all other unnecessary explanations.

t_ch: 怎么有怪物？

t_ch: 这工作好危险啊！
t_en: This job's dangerous!

t_ch: 所以我才只敢放心交给你这种硬核狠人啊！
t_en: That's why I was only confident in entrusting the job to someone as hardcore as you!

t_ch: 忘了跟你说了，现下流云渡一片混乱。有人趁机用货运星槎运送危险品，也有犯罪分子妄图利用货运星槎偷渡……
t_en: I forgot to tell you, Cloudford is in chaos now. Some people take advantage of the opportunity to transport dangerous goods with freight starskiffs, and there are also criminals who try to use freight starskiffs to smuggle people...

t_ch: 所以货运星槎入港的时候，不一定会从里面蹦出什么玩意儿呢！
t_en: So when a freight starskiff enters the port, it's not certain what might pop out of it!

t_ch: 正因如此，现在流云渡才会人手不足啊。只有像你我这样武功高强的人，才能在这个残酷的货运码头生存下去……
t_en: Because of this, Cloudford is now understaffed. Only people with excellent martial arts skills like you and I can survive in this cruel freight jetty...

t_ch: 不多聊了，我回去忙我的工作了。你要小心啊！
t_en: Alright, enough chit-chat. I'm going back to my work. You take care!

t_ch: （这应该就是鹤运物流的时刻表了。确认一下下艘货运星槎的到港时间吧。）
t_en: (This should be the timetable for Heron Express. Confirm the arrival time of the next freight starskiff.)

t_ch: （但是这怎么看都只是鹤运物流的广告牌啊……）

t_ch: 你伸出手，触碰这个「广告牌」。在指间触碰到它的一刹那，你的脑海中传来了一阵温柔的低语。
t_en: You reach out your hand and touch this "billboard." The moment your fingers make contact with it, a gentle voice enters your mind.

t_ch: 「欢迎加入鹤运物流，{NICKNAME}。请开始您本日的工作。」
t_en: "Welcome to Heron Express, {NICKNAME}. Please start your work for the day."

t_ch: 等待下一批货运星槎进港。
t_en: Wait for the next batch of freight starskiffs to enter the port.

t_ch: （不知道会有怎样的货运星槎到港，有点期待了……）
t_en: (I dunno what kinda freight starskiff is gonna dock at the port. But not gonna lie, I'm low-key excited to see what random things they'll bring.)

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: *欠条*
t_en: *IOU document*

t_ch: 抱歉，报酬其实已经准备好了，但是我到处都没有找着能装报酬的宝箱。所以，下次会把这次的报酬一并送过来，放心，少不了你的。
t_en: Apologies, the payment is actually ready, but I couldn't find a chest that can hold the payment. So, I'll send this time's payment along with your next one. Don't worry, I won't leave you dry!

t_en: Some time has passed, and the new incoming freight starskiff awaits pilotage...

t_ch: 你放心这也不难，就像之前一样，你去调整承接器的位置，让这岁阳能量能一口气击穿所有的「标靶气球」就算成功。
t_en: Don't worry, it's not difficult. Just like before, you adjust the position of the receiver so that the heliobus energy can penetrate all the balloon targets in one go.

t_ch: 这「匣里流光」不单单是可以用作武器，光是用在云骑军的金人身上，还可以辅助防御系统和推进系统，真是绝妙的发～明～
t_en: This Luminflux Pyxis can not only be used as a weapon on the Cloud Knights' aurumatons, it can also assist the defense system and propulsion system. It is a wonderful invention~

t_ch: 小娃儿你别光盯着我呀，你去调整下这「匣里流光」的武器系统，测试还没结束嘞。
t_en: Don't just stare at me. Go and adjust the Luminflux Pyxis' weapon system. The test is not over yet.

t_ch: 很好，比我那些不成器的弟子们能干多了。

t_ch: 这岁阳武器经过多次折射，能量也没有衰减，要是用在斗舰编队上，肯定能大展神～威～嚯嚯哈哈哈哈！
t_en: This heliobus weapon has been refracted many times, and its energy has not been attenuated. If it is used in the battleship formation, it will definitely strengthen our power~ Hahaha!

t_ch: 老夫给你准备好了报酬，快收下吧。
t_en: Here's your reward. Take it.

t_ch: 这没有什么不好意思的，劳动创造价值嘛，这是你应得的。
t_en: Don't be shy. Labor creates value. You deserve it.

t_ch: 今天还有武器测试吗？
t_en: Are there weapon tests today?

t_ch: 这岁阳能量的击发器脾气不太好，岁阳能量容器耗尽之后，需要用能量冷凝器进行补给，就像是电池一样，千万不可以勉～强。

t_ch: 再说了，老夫每天能处理的实验数据也是有限的，咱们就先到此为止吧。下次我再给你带点儿更好的东西过来。
t_en: Besides, the experimental data that I can process every day is limited, so let's stop here. I'll bring you something better next time.

t_ch: 今天还有武器测试吗？
t_en: Are there weapon tests today?

t_ch: 老夫就知道你对「匣里流光」感兴趣，哈哈哈哈。正好，老夫刚刚想到了突破容量限制的办法，你刚好可以来试试。
t_en: Hahaha, I knew you would be interested in Luminflux Pyxis. Coincidentally, I just thought of a way to break through its capacity limit. Let's give it a try.

t_ch: 那就来吧！
t_en: Let's do it!

t_ch: 要是我那些蠢货徒弟能有你这样的干劲儿就好了。
t_en: If only my blockheaded apprentices have your drive.

t_ch: 稍等，我还有点事。
t_en: Just a moment, I have something else to do.

t_ch: 行，那等你有空再来。
t_en: That's fine. Come back when you have time.

t_en: In the past, the energy container of the Interastral Peace Corporation would run out of energy after firing fifteen rounds. If we can freely switch between backup energy sources, we can have constant firepower on the battlefield.

t_ch: 老夫得把这个点子记下来……
t_en: I need to get this idea down...

t_ch: 嚯，你这小娃儿不简单啊！已经足够称得上是我公输梁的助手了。
t_en: Oh, you are indeed someone of value and can be my assistant.

t_ch: 要是我的臭徒弟在这里，我肯定就指着他们的鼻子骂起来了——「别人一个外行人都能做到这个程度」！
t_en: If my apprentices were here, I would point at their noses and say to them, "Even an amateur can do this"!

t_ch: 今天还有武器测试吗？
t_en: Are there weapon tests today?

t_ch: 来来来，老夫就很欣赏你这样有动力的年轻人。
t_en: Come here. I love youngsters with motivation like you.

t_ch: 那就来吧！
t_en: Let's do this!

t_ch: 要是我那些蠢货徒弟能有你这样的干劲儿就好了。
t_en: If only my blockheaded apprentices have your drive.

t_ch: 稍等，我还有点事。
t_en: Just a moment, I have something else to do.

t_ch: 行，那等你有空再来。
t_en: That's fine. Come back when you have time.

t_en: Just like the technological structure of the Creation Furnace, the Luminflux Pyxis can also have a fusion furnace, which can stabilize heliobus charged particles at a low speed, thereby increasing the damaging area...

t_en: But if the speed of the particles is decreased, their penetrating power will be reduced. We can't have both. I have to think about it again.

t_ch: 老夫以为今天的测试会难住你，没想到被你三下五除二地解决了。
t_en: I thought that today's test would be difficult for you. Didn't expect you to solve it so quickly.

t_ch: 你下次再来罢，老夫的测试内容可不会一直这么简单。
t_en: Come back next time. My test won't always be so easy.

t_ch: 今天还有武器测试吗？
t_en: Are there weapon tests today?

t_ch: 最近老夫我改进了承接器的工艺，变得可以移动了。这样，未来战场上的云骑军便能够借助脑波，发动无线无人遥距攻击。
t_en: Recently, I have improved the craft of the receiver, and it has become movable. In this way, the Cloud Knights can use brain waves to launch wireless unmanned remote attacks on the battlefield in the future.

t_ch: …说太多你肯定也听不懂吧。没事，你亲自上手试试就知道了。
t_en: You won't be able to understand it through plain words. Try it yourself and you'll know.

t_ch: 那就来吧！
t_en: Let's do it!

t_ch: 要是我那些蠢货徒弟能有你这样的干劲儿就好了。
t_en: If only my blockheaded apprentices have your drive.

t_ch: 稍等，我还有点事。
t_en: Just a moment, I have something else to do.

t_ch: 行，那等你有空再来。
t_en: That's fine. Come back when you have time.

t_en: If we add a force field to bind the particles and twist the inescapable heliobus energy into one strand, it will make the Luminflux Pyxis able to cut anything.

t_ch: 不错，老夫得把这个想法记下来，要是金人能够挥动这样的岁阳武器的话，肯定是所～向～披～靡，无～人～可～挡～
t_en: I will write down this idea. If aurumatons can wield such a heliobus weapon, they will be unstoppable~

t_en: I thought that today's test would be difficult for you. Didn't expect you to solve it so quickly.

t_ch: 你下次再来罢，老夫的测试内容可不会一直这么简单。
t_en: Come back next time. My test won't always be so easy.

t_en: Are there weapon tests today?

t_en: Wait a minute. I have a little inspiration. Leave me alone.

t_ch: 那就来吧！
t_en: Let's do it!

t_en: If only my blockheaded apprentices have your drive.

t_en: Forget about them. Let's get started if you're ready. You are my best assistant!

t_ch: 那就来吧！
t_en: Let's do it!

t_ch: 要是我那些蠢货徒弟能有你这样的干劲儿就好了。
t_en: If only my blockheaded apprentices have your drive.

t_ch: 稍等，我还有点事。
t_en: Just a moment, I have something else to do.

t_ch: 行，那等你有空再来。
t_en: That's fine. Come back when you have time.

t_ch: 要是利用立场将岁阳充能粒子束缚成阵刀形状，那刀锋充满高热的等离子体，破阵杀敌必然无往不利；等到收刀入鞘时再补充岁阳能量……
t_en: If we use a force field to bind heliobus charged particles into the shape of a saber, its blade will be filled with high-heat plasma, making the weapon deadlier. We can replenish the heliobus energy until the saber is sheathed...

t_en: If we make a few more changes to its shape, we can also make knives, spears, swords, axes, halberds, and hooks. In this way, even a large aurumaton can use the Cloud~ Knights'~ combat~ arts~

t_ch: 今天还有武器测试吗？
t_en: Are there weapon tests today?

t_en: Let's get to it!

t_en: If only my blockheaded apprentices have your drive.

t_ch: 稍等，我还有点事。
t_en: Just a moment, I have something else to do.

t_ch: 行，那等你有空再来。

t_en: Compared with the inside of the delve, the combat environment outside the ship must be very complicated. So, heliobus energy weapons may lose shooting accuracy and damage due to environmental factors...

t_ch: 要是这套「匣里流光」能够搭配精神感应系统，根据飞行士直觉自动变换排列排布方式，那样就能将能量收束于一处、增大出力了…真是妙～啊……
t_en: If this set of Luminflux Pyxis can be used with a psychosensory system to automatically change the arrangement according to the pilot's intuition, then the energy can be concentrated in one place and the output can be increased... It's wonderful~

t_en: Hahaha, great. The problem is solved once I saw you. Now I just like you more.

t_en: Are there weapon tests today?

t_en: Let's do it!

t_en: I still feel that the aurumaton's design is a bit backward. As the near-ground levitation technology of the Artisanship Commission matures, why do aurumatons, which are heavy, have to have two feet? Isn't that cumbersome!

t_ch: 依我看，直接装推进器就好了呀！脚只是装饰而已，那帮蠢货怎么就不懂呢……
t_en: In my opinion, we can replace their feet with thrusters directly! The feet are just for decoration. Why don't those fools understand...

t_ch: 不错不错，这次实验数据收集得很顺利…说个题外话可以吗？你觉得咱们工造司的金人造型如何？
t_en: Not bad. The data collection for this experiment went well... Can I digress? What do you think of the shape of the aurumatons in our Artisanship Commission?

t_ch: 感觉还挺好看的。
t_en: They look good.

t_ch: 是吗？有徒弟说，这玩意儿要是能缩放比例做成机巧玩偶，可以赚一大笔经费。
t_en: You think so? Some of my apprentices said that if aurumatons can be scaled to make ingenium dolls, we can make a fortune.

t_ch: 可是我深表怀疑——毕竟它实在是太丑了。
t_en: But I doubt it. After all, they are too ugly.

t_ch: 感觉有点丑。
t_en: They are a bit ugly.

t_ch: 我也觉得。这不就是个有门楼的机械偃偶吗？肯定没人买账的。
t_en: I feel so too. Isn't it just a mechanical puppet with a gate? I don't think anyone would buy it.

t_ch: 今天还有武器测试吗？
t_en: Are there weapon tests today?

t_en: After this test is over, I should go back to the Sapientia Academe to modify the designs. It is thanks to you that the weapon test can go so smoothly. Ready to get started now?

t_ch: 那就来吧！
t_en: Yes!

t_en: If only my blockheaded apprentices have your drive.

t_en: I do have the Golden Cloud Toad and Illumination Dragonfish with me, but it is still not safe to do a weapon test here... In fact, the Cloud Knights have warned me several times before.

t_en: It seems that I was wrong about my apprentices before. It's not that they didn't want to come, they just were not smart enough to evade the Cloud Knights and come to the Artisanship Commission.

t_en: You're more reliable than them. I am going back now, you take care.

t_ch: 还想再继续做测试啊…
t_en: I want to do more tests...

t_ch: 突然来了灵感…
t_en: I suddenly had an inspiration...

t_ch: 今天测试就到这儿吧。

t_en: "Who can develop the best heliobus weapon? Gongshu Liang from the Artisanship Commission!"

t_ch: 好啊，但我不能白干活。
t_en: Okay, but I don't just work for free.

t_ch: 那再好不过了，我还怕你不肯收报酬呢。
t_en: That's wonderful. I was afraid you'd turn down any payment.

t_ch: 我一个外人，这合适吗…
t_en: This isn't my forte, are you sure about this...

t_ch: 就是外人才合适啊。您只需负责启动阵基就好。
t_en: Yes, in fact only outsiders like you are suitable. You just need to start up the base terminal, and that's all.

t_ch: 放心吧，绝不会让你白干。
t_en: Don't worry about it. I'd never let you do it for nothing.

t_ch: 听说你曾经为符玄大人启动过阵基，如何操作应该不需要我多费唇舌了吧。
t_en: I heard you once started up a base terminal for Master Fu Xuan, so I shouldn't have to waste my breath telling you how to operate things.

t_ch: 例行占算并不复杂，让阵法的星纹与昭示图案一致即可。
t_en: Routine divinations are not complicated at all. Just match up the star pattern on the matrix with the prompt.

t_ch: 辛苦了，这是我准备好的报酬。
t_en: Good job. Here's the reward for you, then.

t_ch: 另外，例行占算还会继续…如果{NICKNAME}方便，也请务必拨冗协助。
t_en: Also, I need to keep making more routine divinations... If you don't mind, {NICKNAME}, perhaps you could find the time to help out some more.

t_ch: 我还会再来的。
t_en: I'll come back next time then.

t_ch: 匠人不来了吗？
t_en: What about that craftsman?

t_ch: 有了你的帮助便足够了。
t_en: Your help is more than sufficient.

t_ch: 何况举手之劳的活计，却有报酬可拿，想来您也不会拒绝吧？
t_en: The job is a piece of cake, but it's paid. I don't think you'd want to miss out on that, right?

t_ch: 占算用的方案已准备好了，这就开始？
t_en: The divination plan is ready. Shall we begin?

t_ch: 走吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 请随我来。
t_en: Come with me.

t_ch: 稍等，我还有点事。
t_en: Just a moment. I have something else to do.

t_ch: 无妨，等您方便了之后再来找我吧。
t_en: It's no bother. Come back and see me when it's convenient for you.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，有何贵干？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, what brings you today?

t_ch: 今天占算的对象是什么呢？
t_en: What's the divination target for today?

t_ch: 一道上下波动的曲线罢了，不足为谈。
t_en: It's just a fluctuating curve. Nothing worth mentioning.

t_ch: 到底是什么曲线？
t_en: What exactly is this curve you're talking about?

t_ch: 没想到{NICKNAME}是个好奇心这么重的人呀，呵呵。
t_en: I never thought you'd be such a curious soul, {NICKNAME}. Heehee.

t_ch: 那我就直说了吧，我受某个不愿意透露姓名的老板委托，想预测星际和平公司期货贸易市场的价格波动。
t_en: Then I'll put it bluntly: I was commissioned by a manager who wishes to remain anonymous to predict price fluctuations in the IPC futures trading market.

t_ch: 就这形势，还需要预测？
t_en: Do you need to run predictions for that kind of thing?

t_ch: 大环境确实…不怎么样。但总有人不死心啊。这不，就有人求上门来了？
t_en: The macro-environment is... really not the best. But there's always someone unwilling to give up. As a matter of fact, it appears we have someone now?

t_ch: 大衍穷观阵就算个这玩意儿？
t_en: The Matrix of Prescience Ultima can make predictions about that, too?

t_ch: 风云变幻的行情，不正适合拿来演练穷观阵吗？
t_en: Don't you think ever-changing market conditions are good for training the Matrix of Prescience?

t_ch: 好吧，我就不过问了。
t_en: Alright, I won't ask.

t_ch: 很好，接下来还有一个阵法要布设，走吧。
t_en: Great. There's another matrix to set up. Let's get moving.

t_ch: 走吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 稍等，我还有事。
t_en: Just a moment. I have something else to do.

t_ch: 了解了，如果你还想接着占算，我会在授事厅等着你。
t_en: Understood. If you want to run some more divinations again, I'll be in the Conclave Hall.

t_ch: 占算结果已经拿到，辛苦了{NICKNAME}啦。
t_en: Results obtained. Thanks for your hard work, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你来得正好，我正打算去做例行占算，一起来吧。
t_en: You're just in time. I'm planning to run another routine divination. Let's do it together.

t_ch: 走吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 请随我来。
t_en: Come with me.

t_ch: 稍等，我还有点事。
t_en: Just a moment. I have something else to do.

t_ch: 没事，等你方便了之后再来找我吧。
t_en: All good. Just come find me when it's convenient for you.

t_ch: 有什么事吗？
t_en: What's up?

t_ch: 这次占算对象是什么呢？
t_en: What's the divination target this time?

t_ch: 不过是几个数字的组合罢了。
t_en: It's just a string of numbers.

t_ch: 我非得问问那是什么！
t_en: I've got to ask: What's that!

t_ch: 哎，不知道你是否听说过「亚伯南安星系大乐透」？
t_en: Ah, have you by any chance heard of the Abelane Galaxy Lottery?

t_ch: 他们受常乐天君的神启，每八个标准月随机抽选出某位星系居民的注册编号，开奖的当天让那人变成大富翁。
t_en: Inspired by the Joyseeker, they pick out a random social security number from an Abelane resident once every 8 standardized months, and the jackpot is enough to make you a millionaire.

t_ch: 某位不愿意透露姓名的老板告诉我，他们会根据那组号码提前找到「天选之子」，和那人签订协议、平分奖金，顺便隐去身份，避免被他人盯上。
t_en: One of my managers who wish to remain anonymous told me that they would find the Chosen One of the day before their social security number is drawn out, and sign a contract with them to split the winnings — while helping hide their identity and all the personal targeting that comes with that.

t_ch: 大乐透早就定好了谁会中奖吧。
t_en: They've already picked out the next lottery winner, right?

t_ch: 据说那组数字是靠侦测引力波变化来敲定的绝对真随机。不然人家也不会求上门来了。
t_en: Rumor has it that the number set is determined by fluctuations in gravity waves, meaning it is truly a random number. Otherwise, people wouldn't be coming to look for me, would they?

t_ch: 不过对穷观阵来说，真随机也好，有人弄虚作假也罢，只要老板提供的信息足够，结果必然准确。
t_en: It doesn't matter to the Matrix of Prescience though whether a number is random or manipulated by someone. So long as I get enough data from this manager, the result is guaranteed to be accurate.

t_ch: 将穷人的钱聚集起来赌运气？
t_en: So it's pooling together money from the poor to gamble?

t_ch: 人类嘛，「损不足而奉有余嘛」。
t_en: Humans. We mitigate our losses and share our surplus.

t_ch: 往好了想吧，在短生种短到不可思议的生命里，能有机会翻身过几天暴富的日子，哪怕就几天，这盼头也足够他们去奔命了。
t_en: Thinking over it more positively, short-life species have such ridiculously short lives that a chance to be a millionaire — even if only for a few days — is enough to keep them going at it.

t_ch: 彩票不是捕捉穿越者的陷阱吗？
t_en: Isn't the lottery really just bait to apprehend illegal time travelers?

t_ch: 噗，你这人脑袋里转的念头可真有意思。
t_en: Ha, I'd love to take a vacation in your brain. What an idea!

t_ch: 不过作为卜者，咱们接受的第一堂课就告诉我们：时间旅行是不可能成立的。
t_en: But as a diviner, the first lesson we ever learn tells us that time travel is impossible.

t_ch: 好吧，我就不过问了。
t_en: Alright, I won't ask.

t_ch: 占算结果已经拿到，辛苦了。
t_en: Results obtained. Thanks for your hard work.

t_ch: 这些内容都和太卜司无关？
t_en: So these have nothing to do at all with the Divination Commission?

t_ch: 怎么会？既然用上了穷观阵，便是为它累积案例，为更准确地卜筮提供经验。
t_en: Why would they? I'm using the Matrix of Prescience to build up its databanks, and provide more experience to calibrate more accurate divinations.

t_ch: 怎么能说和太卜司无关？
t_en: How could you say it has nothing to do with the Divination Commission?

t_ch: 下回还会有占算的活计，您可别忘了。
t_en: Next time there'll be more routine divinations. Don't forget.

t_ch: 我告辞了。
t_en: See you later, then.

t_ch: 下回还会有占算的活计，您可别忘了。
t_en: Next time there'll be more routine divinations. Don't forget.

t_en: What exactly do you mean by a sports match?

t_ch: 不知道{NICKNAME}是否听过「泰科铵机动球」？
t_en: Have you ever heard of a game called Taikiyan Roboball, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 那是一种融合了竞速与对抗的运动比赛，运动员佩戴着护甲与武器在复杂的立体赛道上争夺比分，还有自己的性命。
t_en: It's a sport that combines racing with combat. Players wear body armor and use weapons to fight for points — and their lives — on this complicated 3D track.

t_ch: 某位不愿意透露姓名的老板告诉我，只要能帮他预测出比赛结果，他就能够赚得一笔可观的收入…而我们也能从中分得一杯羹。
t_en: One owner, who wishes to remain anonymous, told me he could make a considerable fortune if I could help him predict match results... And we'd take a cut of that.

t_ch: 穷观阵真是大材小用。
t_en: This really is overkill for the Matrix of Prescience.

t_ch: 是是，您批评的是…不过既然是例行占算，校准阵法，算算球赛也无伤大雅嘛。
t_en: Yeah, what you're criticizing is... It's just routine divination that helps calibrate the matrix. We're not harming anyone.

t_ch: 是啊，又有活来了。{NICKNAME}你也对这一套熟门熟路了。
t_en: Yes, there's another job on. {NICKNAME}, you're an old hand at this now.
